---
title: 'A fragile recovery: Rural employment set back twice since 2007'
description: Line chart showing relative change in employment since the Great Recession,
  comparing rural and nonrural areas.
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-emp-change-since-gr-lc/
categories:
  - Chart
  - Labor Market
  - Rural Development
tags:
  - QCEW
  - Employment
  - Rural vs. Nonrural
  - County-Level
  - Post-Great Recession
  - Line Chart
image: 3.1_emp_change_since_GR_lc.svg
chart:
  title: 'A fragile recovery: Rural employment set back twice since 2007'
  chartType: line
  interactive: false
  dataSource: Bureau of Labor Statistics, Quarterly Census of Employment and Wages
  sourceUrl: https://www.bls.gov/cew/
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - qcew-employment-wages
usesPackages:
  - cori-data-bds
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![This chart tracks employment levels in rural and nonrural counties relative to 2007 (pre-Great Recession) baseline. It reveals that rural areas experienced deeper employment losses and slower recovery compared to nonrural areas.](./3.1_emp_change_since_GR_lc.svg)

## Overview

This chart tracks employment levels in rural and nonrural counties relative to 2007 (pre-Great Recession) baseline. It reveals that rural areas experienced deeper employment losses and slower recovery compared to nonrural areas.

## Key Findings

- Rural employment has not fully recovered to pre-2007 levels
- Nonrural areas recovered and exceeded 2007 employment by approximately 15%
- The COVID-19 pandemic caused a second major setback to rural employment recovery

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/emp_change_lc.R` in the producing project.
